findings.title: Quote sanitization now reintroduces quotes
findings.evidence: The old code removed double quotes by replacing them with apostrophes, but the new replacement string contains a literal double quote at runtime (`\'\"\\`). Inputs containing `"` will still produce output containing `"`, which regresses quote removal and can break callers that rely on quote-free cleaned strings.
findings.suggested_comment: This replacement still emits a literal double quote, so `Process("a\"b")` no longer produces quote-free output. If the contract is to sanitize/remove double quotes as before, this should replace `"` with a value that does not contain `"` or use context-appropriate escaping at the actual sink.
findings.fix_hint: In Go, `"\'\"\\"` evaluates to characters including a double quote. Add/adjust a test for input containing `"` and decide whether quotes should be removed or escaped for a specific output context.
